constraints: Save Assign before trial; rollback after cost check.
Fallback inserts every order.
content:
Skill: nearest_first
When: selecting which order to insert next.
Do: pick unserved order with minimum cal_dis() to any existing route. Try all Assigns, insert at position with smallest cost_delta after GenRoute().
Fallback: if no feasible position, create new Assign and force AddShip.
Safety: save Assign state before trial insert; RemoveShip + GenRoute to undo after cost check.
summary: Low-density d25 heuristic: select unserved order closest to any route, insert at min-cost position.
title: Nearest Insertion Skill Card

constraints: Separate selection and insertion criteria.
Fallback inserts every order.
content:
Skill: farthest_first
When: orders are geographically dispersed (low/medium density).
Do: pick unserved order with MAXIMUM minimum-distance to any existing route. Insert at best-cost position across all Assigns.
Fallback: force-insert into existing/new Assign if no feasible position.
Safety: selection criterion (farthest) and insertion criterion (cheapest) are separate steps.
summary: Low-density dispersed heuristic: select order farthest from any route first, then insert at min-cost position.
title: Farthest Insertion Skill Card

constraints: Default alpha1=1, alpha2=0.
GenRoute after each trial add/remove.
content:
Skill: solomon_select
When: medium-to-high density, need global selection criterion.
Do: for each unserved order k: score = alpha1cal_dis(depot,k) - alpha2cost_delta_of_best_insertion. Select order with max score. Insert at its best position.
Fallback: if no feasible insertion, greedy force-insert any order.
Safety: default alpha1=1, alpha2=0. GenRoute after every trial add/remove.
summary: For d50/d75 medium-to-high density, use a global weighted-score selection criterion with cost-delta insertion.
title: Solomon I1 Skill Card

constraints: If one feasible position, insert now.
GenRoute after each trial pair.
content:
Skill: regret2_select
When: multiple orders compete for limited route capacity (d50+ density).
Do: for each unserved order, compute best and second-best insertion cost_delta across all Assigns. regret = cost_2nd - cost_1st. Select order with max regret. Insert at best position.
Fallback: if only one feasible position exists, insert immediately.
Safety: GenRoute after every trial AddShip/RemoveShip pair for accurate cost_delta.
summary: For d50+ medium density and limited route capacity, use lookahead regret2 insertion before route slots vanish.
title: Regret-2 Insertion Skill Card

constraints: Track served set.
Never insert same order twice.
content:
Skill: savings_merge
When: deciding whether to add order to existing Assign or start new one.
Do: compute s(i,j)=cal_dis(depot,i)+cal_dis(depot,j)-cal_dis(i,j). Sort pairs by s descending. Merge highest-savings pairs into same Assign. Remaining unserved: greedy insert.
Fallback: orders with no positive-savings partner get their own Assign.
Safety: track served set; never insert same order twice.
summary: Route-consolidation heuristic: rank pair-savings, merge strong pairs into shared Assigns, then insert leftovers.
title: Clarke-Wright Savings Skill Card

constraints: Return one score per feasible bin.
Prefer low used-bin count over cosmetic residual balance.
content:
Skill: obp_first_fit
When: need a simple online packing baseline.
Do: give earlier feasible bins higher scores so the first feasible bin is selected.
Fallback: if remaining is empty, evaluator opens a new bin.
Safety: return len(remaining) finite scores only.
summary: Online bin packing baseline: prefer the earliest feasible bin to reduce new-bin creation.
title: OBP First Fit Skill Card

constraints: Return one score per feasible bin.
Tie-break deterministically by index.
content:
Skill: obp_best_fit
When: objective is to minimize used bins in online bin packing.
Do: score each feasible bin by how full it becomes after placing item; prefer smallest residual capacity rem-item.
Fallback: if no bin is feasible, evaluator opens a new bin.
Safety: never score infeasible bins; remaining is already feasible.
summary: Best-fit scoring: prefer feasible bin with minimum residual capacity after placing the item.
title: OBP Best Fit Skill Card

constraints: Return finite scores.
Useful when small gaps should stay available.
content:
Skill: obp_worst_fit
When: many medium items arrive and over-tight packing may hurt later placements.
Do: prefer feasible bin with largest residual capacity after placing item; keep bins balanced instead of full.
Fallback: switch toward best-fit for large items above capacity/2.
Safety: output exactly len(remaining) scores.
summary: Worst-fit scoring: prefer larger residual capacity to keep bins balanced for later items.
title: OBP Worst Fit Skill Card

constraints: Keep score formula simple and bounded.
Return len(remaining) scores.
content:
Skill: obp_harmonic
When: item sizes vary across small, medium, and large classes.
Do: bucket item by size/capacity ratio; for large items use best-fit, for small items preserve medium gaps, for medium items penalize residual below item/2.
Fallback: default to best-fit score.
Safety: no randomness or lookahead.
summary: Harmonic-style scoring: adapt bin preference by item size class relative to capacity.
title: OBP Harmonic Skill Card

constraints: Do not use future items.
Keep polynomial numerically stable.
content:
Skill: obp_residual_poly
When: best-fit leaves many unusable tiny gaps.
Do: compute residual r=rem-item; reward tight fit but add penalty for residual ranges that are likely unusable, e.g. r near small isolated gaps.
Fallback: if formula ties, use best-fit.
Safety: scores must be finite for every feasible bin.
summary: FunSearch-style residual penalty: combine tight-fit reward with polynomial penalty on unusable gaps.
title: OBP FunSearch Residual Polynomial Skill Card

constraints: Use math functions only if stable.
Return finite scores for every feasible bin.
content:
Skill: obp_util_sqrt_exp
When: need smooth scoring beyond pure best-fit.
Do: combine utilization gain, sqrt-normalized residual, and exp decay penalty for awkward gaps. Prefer high utilization without creating many tiny unusable spaces.
Fallback: clamp to best-fit style score if expression is unstable.
Safety: avoid NaN/Inf.
summary: EoH-style smooth score: mix utilization, sqrt residual terms, and exp decay gap penalty.
title: OBP EoH Utilization Sqrt Exp Skill Card

constraints: Return one unvisited node id.
Keep deterministic and bounded.
content:
Skill: tsp_nearest_neighbor
When: constructing a TSP tour step by step.
Do: choose unvisited node with minimum distance from current_node. Use distance_matrix[current_node][unvisited_nodes] and argmin.
Fallback: if only one node remains, return it.
Safety: return an int contained in unvisited_nodes.
summary: TSP baseline: select nearest unvisited node from current_node.
title: TSP Nearest Neighbor Skill Card

constraints: Return one unvisited node id.
Avoid expensive full-route simulation.
content:
Skill: tsp_nearest_insertion
When: nearest neighbor may create long final edges.
Do: score candidate u by distance current->u plus u->destination, favoring nodes that are close to both current path and return direction.
Fallback: use nearest neighbor if scores tie.
Safety: compute only over unvisited_nodes.
summary: TSP insertion-aware selection: balance current distance and return-to-destination distance.
title: TSP Nearest Insertion Skill Card

constraints: Return one unvisited node id.
Do not revisit nodes.
content:
Skill: tsp_farthest_insertion
When: distant clusters may be left until too late.
Do: prefer candidate with larger distance from destination or current cluster, then choose the least damaging such node. Seed far regions early.
Fallback: nearest neighbor for small remaining sets.
Safety: apply only to unvisited_nodes.
summary: TSP farthest-style selection: visit distant clusters earlier to avoid isolated tail costs.
title: TSP Farthest Insertion Skill Card

constraints: Keep lookahead bounded.
Return one valid node.
content:
Skill: tsp_regret_insertion
When: several candidate nodes compete and one may become costly later.
Do: for each candidate, compare best and second-best local connection alternatives; regret = second_best - best. Prefer high regret with moderate current distance.
Fallback: nearest neighbor if regret is flat.
Safety: avoid O(n^3) loops.
summary: TSP regret selection: prioritize nodes whose alternatives worsen quickly.
title: TSP Regret Insertion Skill Card

constraints: Do not perform full 2-opt search inside selector.
Keep output a single node id.
content:
Skill: tsp_two_opt_awareness
When: a nearest step may create crossing or long edges.
Do: penalize candidates that create very long current->u edge relative to local neighbor distances; prefer choices that keep route locally smooth.
Fallback: nearest neighbor.
Safety: selector remains greedy and bounded.
summary: TSP 2-opt-aware selection: avoid locally long edges likely to create crossings.
title: TSP 2-opt Awareness Skill Card

constraints: Return depot only to close a route.
Use unvisited_nodes already filtered by capacity.
content:
Skill: cvrp_nearest_capacity
When: selecting next feasible CVRP customer.
Do: prefer smallest distance from current_node. Capacity is a feasibility filter, not a scoring term. Do not add demand/rest_capacity to the score.
Fallback: return depot if no feasible unvisited node.
Safety: return unvisited node or depot only.
summary: CVRP nearest-first: select closest feasible customer, treat capacity as constraint not score.
title: CVRP Nearest Capacity Skill Card

constraints: Keep savings formula bounded.
Return one feasible customer or depot.
content:
Skill: cvrp_savings
When: choosing customers that consolidate routes.
Do: score u by savings = d(depot,current)+d(depot,u)-d(current,u). Prefer high savings; demand feasibility is binary.
Fallback: nearest feasible customer.
Safety: do not choose infeasible demand.
summary: CVRP savings: score by Clarke-Wright savings, demand is binary feasibility only.
title: CVRP Savings Skill Card

constraints: Use simple geometric proxy only.
Return one feasible customer.
content:
Skill: cvrp_sweep
When: customers are spatially clustered around depot.
Do: prefer nodes with similar polar angle from depot as current_node, then nearest distance; this keeps routes in angular sectors.
Fallback: nearest capacity if angle is unavailable.
Safety: use deterministic atan2 scoring only.
summary: CVRP sweep-style selection: keep each route within a depot-centered angular sector.
title: CVRP Sweep Skill Card

constraints: Bound lookahead to feasible nodes.
Do not mutate arrays.
content:
Skill: cvrp_regret_insertion
When: several candidates compete and some may become hard to reach later.
Do: compute best and second-best distance/detour from current_node; regret = second_best - best. Prefer high regret customers that would otherwise force a long detour.
Fallback: nearest feasible customer if regrets are flat.
Safety: use rest_capacity for feasibility check only.
summary: CVRP regret: prefer customers with high detour regret, capacity is feasibility not score.
title: CVRP Regret Insertion Skill Card

constraints: Respect rest_capacity.
Avoid returning depot too early without reason.
content:
Skill: cvrp_far_first
When: near depot or starting a new route.
Do: prefer distant unvisited customers to seed routes toward clusters far from depot. Use distance_matrix[depot][u] to measure distance from depot; prefer larger values early in the route, then switch to nearest once away from depot.
Fallback: nearest feasible customer if far nodes exhausted.
Safety: never select customer with demand > rest_capacity.
summary: CVRP far-first: seed routes toward distant clusters, switch to nearest once en route.
title: CVRP Far First Skill Card

constraints: Return exactly one int from unvisited_nodes.
Never return a visited node or destination_node.
Do not mutate unvisited_nodes or distance_matrix.
Keep computation bounded and deterministic.
content:
Skill: tsp_evolved_adaptive_weights_destination_forward_score
When: early tour steps should favor exploration, late steps should favor return; the tour must return to a destination/depot and the last edge is costly; direct connection cost is the primary selection signal
Do: use remaining_ratio to dynamically adjust forward vs backward weights; score each candidate by d(current,u) + α·d(u,dest); increase α as fewer nodes remain; weight the direct distance from current node to candidate; normalize forward and backward distances to [0,1] before combining
Fallback: nearest neighbor if scores tie or few nodes remain.
Safety: return one valid node; do not mutate inputs; keep computation bounded.
summary: TSP construction heuristic evolved from best code: destination + normalize + adaptive_weights.
title: TSP Destination Normalize Adaptive Weights Evolved Card

constraints: Return exactly one int from unvisited_nodes.
Never return a visited node or destination_node.
Do not mutate unvisited_nodes or distance_matrix.
Keep computation bounded and deterministic.
content:
Skill: tsp_evolved_adaptive_weights_centrality_destination
When: early tour steps should favor exploration, late steps should favor return; some nodes are more central and should be visited strategically; the tour must return to a destination/depot and the last edge is costly
Do: use remaining_ratio to dynamically adjust forward vs backward weights; prefer nodes with high closeness centrality or high MST edge weight; score each candidate by d(current,u) + α·d(u,dest); increase α as fewer nodes remain; weight the direct distance from current node to candidate
Fallback: nearest neighbor if scores tie or few nodes remain.
Safety: return one valid node; do not mutate inputs; keep computation bounded.
summary: TSP construction heuristic evolved from best code: regret + destination + normalize.
title: TSP Regret Destination Normalize Evolved Card

constraints: Return exactly one int from unvisited_nodes.
Never return a visited node or destination_node.
Do not mutate unvisited_nodes or distance_matrix.
Keep computation bounded and deterministic.
content:
Skill: tsp_evolved_centrality_destination_farthest
When: some nodes are more central and should be visited strategically; the tour must return to a destination/depot and the last edge is costly; distant clusters may be left until too late
Do: prefer nodes with high closeness centrality or high MST edge weight; score each candidate by d(current,u) + α·d(u,dest); increase α as fewer nodes remain; seed distant clusters/regions early; prefer candidates far from current position; weight the direct distance from current node to candidate
Fallback: nearest neighbor if scores tie or few nodes remain.
Safety: return one valid node; do not mutate inputs; keep computation bounded.
summary: TSP construction heuristic evolved from best code: regret + farthest + destination.
title: TSP Regret Farthest Destination Evolved Card

constraints: Return one int from unvisited_nodes, or depot only when intentionally ending the route.
Never return an infeasible node (demand > rest_capacity).
Do not mutate unvisited_nodes, demands, or distance_matrix.
content:
Skill: cvrp_evolved_capacity_destination_savings
When: vehicle capacity constrains which customers can be served; the tour must return to a destination/depot and the last edge is costly; merging separate trips can reduce total distance
Do: filter to feasible candidates that fit remaining vehicle capacity; score each candidate by d(current,u) + α·d(u,dest); increase α as fewer nodes remain; compute savings S(i,j) = d(ref,i)+d(ref,j)-d(i,j); merge by highest savings
Fallback: nearest neighbor if scores tie or few nodes remain.
Safety: return one valid node; do not mutate inputs; keep computation bounded.
summary: CVRP construction heuristic evolved from best code: destination + savings.
title: CVRP Destination Savings Evolved Card

constraints: Return one int from unvisited_nodes, or depot only when intentionally ending the route.
Never return an infeasible node (demand > rest_capacity).
Do not mutate unvisited_nodes, demands, or distance_matrix.
content:
Skill: cvrp_evolved_capacity_clustering_destination
When: vehicle capacity constrains which customers can be served; unvisited nodes form spatial clusters; the tour must return to a destination/depot and the last edge is costly
Do: filter to feasible candidates that fit remaining vehicle capacity; identify unvisited node clusters; visit distant clusters before nearby ones; score each candidate by d(current,u) + α·d(u,dest); increase α as fewer nodes remain; consider 2-step lookahead; penalize choices that strand distant nodes
Fallback: nearest neighbor if scores tie or few nodes remain.
Safety: return one valid node; do not mutate inputs; keep computation bounded.
summary: CVRP construction heuristic evolved from best code: destination + clustering + savings.
title: CVRP Destination Clustering Savings Evolved Card

constraints: Return one int from unvisited_nodes, or depot only when intentionally ending the route.
Never return an infeasible node (demand > rest_capacity).
Do not mutate unvisited_nodes, demands, or distance_matrix.
content:
Skill: cvrp_evolved_capacity_destination_lookahead
When: vehicle capacity constrains which customers can be served; the tour must return to a destination/depot and the last edge is costly; greedy choices can strand distant nodes
Do: filter to feasible candidates that fit remaining vehicle capacity; score each candidate by d(current,u) + α·d(u,dest); increase α as fewer nodes remain; consider 2-step lookahead; penalize choices that strand distant nodes; select the unvisited node with minimum distance from current node
Fallback: nearest neighbor if scores tie or few nodes remain.
Safety: return one valid node; do not mutate inputs; keep computation bounded.
summary: CVRP construction heuristic evolved from best code: regret + destination + normalize.
title: CVRP Regret Destination Normalize Evolved Card

constraints: Return one int from unvisited_nodes, or depot only when intentionally ending the route.
Never return an infeasible node (demand > rest_capacity).
Do not mutate unvisited_nodes, demands, or distance_matrix.
content:
Skill: cvrp_evolved_adaptive_weights_capacity_destination
When: early tour steps should favor exploration, late steps should favor return; vehicle capacity constrains which customers can be served; the tour must return to a destination/depot and the last edge is costly
Do: use remaining_ratio to dynamically adjust forward vs backward weights; filter to feasible candidates that fit remaining vehicle capacity; score each candidate by d(current,u) + α·d(u,dest); increase α as fewer nodes remain; consider 2-step lookahead; penalize choices that strand distant nodes
Fallback: nearest neighbor if scores tie or few nodes remain.
Safety: return one valid node; do not mutate inputs; keep computation bounded.
summary: CVRP construction heuristic evolved from best code: regret + destination + normalize.
title: CVRP Regret Destination Normalize Evolved Card

constraints: Return one int from unvisited_nodes, or depot only when intentionally ending the route.
Never return an infeasible node (demand > rest_capacity).
Do not mutate unvisited_nodes, demands, or distance_matrix.
content:
Skill: cvrp_evolved_capacity_destination_farthest
When: vehicle capacity constrains which customers can be served; the tour must return to a destination/depot and the last edge is costly; distant clusters may be left until too late
Do: filter to feasible candidates that fit remaining vehicle capacity; score each candidate by d(current,u) + α·d(u,dest); increase α as fewer nodes remain; seed distant clusters/regions early; prefer candidates far from current position; consider 2-step lookahead; penalize choices that strand distant nodes
Fallback: nearest neighbor if scores tie or few nodes remain.
Safety: return one valid node; do not mutate inputs; keep computation bounded.
summary: CVRP construction heuristic evolved from best code: farthest + destination + normalize.
title: CVRP Farthest Destination Normalize Evolved Card

constraints: Return one int from unvisited_nodes, or depot only when intentionally ending the route.
Never return an infeasible node (demand > rest_capacity).
Do not mutate unvisited_nodes, demands, or distance_matrix.
content:
Skill: cvrp_evolved_capacity_clustering_destination
When: vehicle capacity constrains which customers can be served; unvisited nodes form spatial clusters; the tour must return to a destination/depot and the last edge is costly
Do: filter to feasible candidates that fit remaining vehicle capacity; identify unvisited node clusters; visit distant clusters before nearby ones; score each candidate by d(current,u) + α·d(u,dest); increase α as fewer nodes remain; seed distant clusters/regions early; prefer candidates far from current position
Fallback: nearest neighbor if scores tie or few nodes remain.
Safety: return one valid node; do not mutate inputs; keep computation bounded.
summary: CVRP construction heuristic evolved from best code: regret + farthest + destination.
title: CVRP Regret Farthest Destination Evolved Card

constraints: Return one int from unvisited_nodes, or depot only when intentionally ending the route.
Never return an infeasible node (demand > rest_capacity).
Do not mutate unvisited_nodes, demands, or distance_matrix.
content:
Skill: cvrp_evolved_adaptive_weights_capacity_destination
When: early tour steps should favor exploration, late steps should favor return; vehicle capacity constrains which customers can be served; the tour must return to a destination/depot and the last edge is costly
Do: use remaining_ratio to dynamically adjust forward vs backward weights; filter to feasible candidates that fit remaining vehicle capacity; score each candidate by d(current,u) + α·d(u,dest); increase α as fewer nodes remain; seed distant clusters/regions early; prefer candidates far from current position
Fallback: nearest neighbor if scores tie or few nodes remain.
Safety: return one valid node; do not mutate inputs; keep computation bounded.
summary: CVRP construction heuristic evolved from best code: regret + farthest + destination.
title: CVRP Regret Farthest Destination Evolved Card

constraints: Return one int from unvisited_nodes, or depot only when intentionally ending the route.
Never return an infeasible node (demand > rest_capacity).
Do not mutate unvisited_nodes, demands, or distance_matrix.
content:
Skill: cvrp_evolved_capacity_destination_normalize
When: vehicle capacity constrains which customers can be served; the tour must return to a destination/depot and the last edge is costly; forward and backward distances are on different scales
Do: filter to feasible candidates that fit remaining vehicle capacity; score each candidate by d(current,u) + α·d(u,dest); increase α as fewer nodes remain; normalize forward and backward distances to [0,1] before combining; adapt strategy based on how many nodes remain
Fallback: nearest neighbor if scores tie or few nodes remain.
Safety: return one valid node; do not mutate inputs; keep computation bounded.
summary: CVRP construction heuristic evolved from best code: destination + normalize.
title: CVRP Destination Normalize Evolved Card

constraints: Never return an infeasible node.
Use depot distance only as a route-seeding signal.
content:
Skill: history_cvrp_far_destination_seed
When: starting a route from depot or when distant clusters risk being delayed.
Do: maximize distance_matrix[depot][u] among feasible candidates to seed far clusters, then switch back to the paired literature card for route continuation.
Fallback: nearest feasible customer if far candidates tie.
Safety: return one feasible unvisited node or depot only when closing route.
summary: CVRP history operator: seed routes with far feasible customers, then hand off to simpler continuation logic.
title: CVRP History Far Destination Seed Card

constraints: Capacity is a hard feasibility filter.
Do not add demand/rest_capacity into the score.
content:
Skill: history_cvrp_capacity_feasible_filter
When: rest_capacity restricts which customers can be served.
Do: first filter candidates with demands[u] <= rest_capacity, then let the paired strategy score only feasible nodes. Keep capacity out of the objective score.
Fallback: return depot if no feasible unvisited customer remains.
Safety: never select demand > rest_capacity.
summary: CVRP history operator: use capacity only as feasibility filtering before distance/regret scoring.
title: CVRP History Capacity Feasible Filter Card

constraints: Score direction must stay explicit.
Keep alpha bounded between 0 and 1.
content:
Skill: history_cvrp_remaining_aware_alpha
When: route progress should shift from exploration to cleanup.
Do: compute remaining_ratio = len(unvisited_nodes)/total_nodes. Use alpha = min(1.0, max(0.0, remaining_ratio)) to weight exploration early and reduce it near the end.
Fallback: fixed alpha=0.5 if total_nodes is unavailable.
Safety: alpha changes weights only; feasibility still comes first.
summary: CVRP history operator: adjust a bounded alpha by remaining customer ratio.
title: CVRP History Remaining-Aware Alpha Card
